figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

  "title": "解决方案 ——将UA 与留存环节打通，释放整体价值。",
  "page_index": 47,
  "page_range": "47"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0083",
    "title": "创意瓶颈",
    "page_range": "47",
    "summary": "This section addresses the \"creative bottleneck\" in app marketing and monetization, providing quantitative benchmarks to answer questions about acquisition costs, user conversion, and profitability timelines.\n- **Topics & Claims:** Global CPI is continuously rising across nearly all app categories; overall user monetization rate is under 5%; casual app monetization is below 2%; current break-even period exceeds 90 days.\n- **Entities & Metrics:** 全球CPI (Global Cost Per Install), 休闲类应用 (Casual apps), 变现用户比例 (Monetization rate), 盈亏平衡周期 (Break-even period); specific metrics: <5%, <2%, >90 days.\n- **Dates/Timeframes:** Current break-even cycle duration (>90 days).\n- **Evidence Type:** Statistical market data with a cited reference (footnote ²) for the casual app metric.\n- **Retrieval Utility:** Answers queries on current CPI trends, category-specific monetization rates, profitability timelines, and economic pressures defining creative bottlenecks. Contains no definitions, procedures, tables, or figures."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0084",
    "title": "成本膨胀",
    "page_range": "47",
    "summary": "This section addresses the topic of cost inflation in data acquisition and digital advertising. It can answer retrieval queries regarding:\n- Topics & Claims: How tightening global privacy regulations drive up operational costs; the increasing difficulty of obtaining user consent; and the declining consumer tolerance for intrusive advertising.\n- Entities: Global privacy regulations, data collection/storage/sharing practices, user consent, intrusive advertising.\n- Dates, Metrics, Tables, Figures, Procedures, Definitions, Evidence Types: Not specified in this excerpt.\nThe text functions as a high-level claim linking regulatory compliance burdens and shifting user preferences to rising data acquisition costs, suitable for questions on privacy law impacts, ad-tech cost drivers, and consumer consent challenges."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0085",
    "title": "隐私约束",
    "page_range": "47",
    "summary": "This section addresses privacy constraints in digital advertising, enabling retrieval for questions regarding:\n- **Topic & Entities:** How Apple’s App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework impacts user-level tracking visibility.\n- **Metrics:** The approximate global user opt-in rate for tracking (~35%).\n- **Claims & Implications:** Why most advertisers must rely on incomplete signals for campaign optimization due to low tracking consent.\n- **Evidence Type:** Statistical data (referenced via footnote) on user tracking preferences.\nThe section does not contain dates, procedural steps, formal definitions, tables, or figures."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0086",
    "title": "信号流失",
    "page_range": "47-48",
    "summary": "This section addresses the topic of signal loss (信号流失) in digital advertising and can answer retrieval questions regarding:\n- **Topics/Context:** The financial and competitive impact of signal loss on advertising strategies.\n- **Entities:** Avi Das, Chief Revenue Officer at Aarki.\n- **Claims:** Blindly increasing ad investment wastes budget and causes advertisers to bid against themselves.\n- **Procedures/Strategies:** The recommended approach is to redesign the collaboration/synergy between UA and RT rather than increasing spend, in order to achieve smarter and more effective growth.\n- **Evidence Types:** Expert industry quote/claim.\n- **Answerable Questions:** What are the risks of blind budget increases during signal loss? What strategic shift does Aarki’s leadership recommend for UA and RT? How should growth tactics be adjusted amid signal loss?\n- **Absent Elements:** No dates, formal definitions, tables, figures, or quantitative metrics are provided in this excerpt."
  }
]

message.content:
This section (Page 47) outlines the strategic imperative to integrate User Acquisition (UA) and Retention (RT) to overcome systemic app marketing challenges. It synthesizes four key subtopics: Creative Bottleneck, Cost Inflation, Privacy Constraints, and Signal Loss.
- **Topics & Claims:** Global CPI is continuously rising; overall user monetization is under 5% (casual apps <2%); break-even periods exceed 90 days. Tightening global privacy regulations, declining user consent, and reduced tolerance for intrusive ads are inflating data acquisition costs. Apple’s ATT framework limits tracking visibility, forcing reliance on incomplete optimization signals. Blindly increasing ad spend during signal loss wastes budget and triggers self-bidding; the recommended strategic shift is to redesign UA-RT collaboration rather than increasing spend.
- **Entities:** Global privacy regulations, Apple App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework, casual apps, and Avi Das (Chief Revenue Officer at Aarki).
- **Metrics:** <5% overall monetization rate, <2% casual app monetization rate, >90-day break-even cycle, ~35% global tracking opt-in rate.
- **Evidence Types:** Statistical market data (referenced via footnotes) and expert industry quotes/claims.
- **Absent Elements:** No specific dates, tables, figures, formal definitions, or step-by-step procedures are present in this excerpt.
- **Retrieval Utility:** Optimized for queries on current app marketing economic pressures, privacy regulation impacts on ad-tech costs, ATT tracking consent benchmarks, financial risks of signal loss, and strategic recommendations for UA-RT integration.
